data.title: Goldman Sachs CEO David Solomon Supports Clarity Act for Digital Asset Regulation
data.description:
Goldman Sachs Chairman and Chief Executive David Solomon publicly backed the Clarity Act, which seeks to create a new regulatory framework for the digital asset market. According to Foresight News, Solomon said in an interview that the bill is not perfect, but its importance lies in creating a level playing field, improving market stability, and allowing these markets to develop properly. He added that he strongly supports moving the bill forward.

The comments came as Republican senators began circulating a new draft of the bill and planned a full Senate vote next week. The stance contrasts with opposition from other bankers, including JPMorgan Chase Chief Executive Jamie Dimon. Critics say the bill could create an unfair advantage for crypto companies that offer stablecoin yield rewards, potentially posing risks to consumer banks. The American Bankers Association, the Bank Policy Institute, and other industry groups have also said the bill's stablecoin provisions threaten local credit that supports U.S. economic activity.

The legislation also includes ethics provisions that would prohibit federal officials from issuing their own tokens. Democratic senators said the revised text does not offer enough concessions and are expected to continue pushing for changes before the vote.

data.title: Gorae To Put 50,000 EV Chargers On-Chain In South Korea This Year
data.description:
Sui said electric vehicle charging project Gorae will bring more than 50,000 charging stations on-chain in South Korea this year through cooperation with Sui and the decentralized storage network Walrus. According to Foresight News, Gorae is working with one of South Korea's largest charging station operators to use Sui for payment settlement and Walrus to record each charging session as verifiable data in the same transaction.

Gorae plans to begin a phased rollout of South Korea's network on-chain in the second half of 2026, then expand to Asia-Pacific, Europe and North America.

data.title: Capita Economics: Gulf Recovery Faces New Setback as Iran Attacks Raise Downside Risks
data.description: According to Jin10, Capita Economics assistant economist Nicolas Crittenden said a new round of Iranian attacks could undermine the Gulf region's early economic recovery. He added that the latest escalation could reverse the improvement seen after last month's U.S.-Iran ceasefire eased disruptions to regional trade and energy supplies. Crittenden said private-sector activity across the Gulf improved in June, led by Saudi Arabia, while exports to the region from major trading partners also increased and flight numbers returned to near pre-conflict levels, signaling a recovery in tourism and logistics. He said renewed disruption to traffic through the Strait of Hormuz could stall the recovery, and downside risks are building around Capita Economics' forecast that Gulf GDP will shrink by about 6% this year.

data.title: STOCKS | Apple Maps to Be Integrated Into Ford Vehicles' UEV Platform in 2027
data.description: According to Jin10, Apple Maps will be installed on Ford vehicles' UEV electronic platform in 2027.

data.title: DeFi Tokenized RWA Use Returns to About $3.77 Billion After KelpDAO Exploit
data.description: Active use of tokenized real-world assets in DeFi has returned to about $3.77 billion, nearly reaching the level seen before the April 18 KelpDAO exploit. According to NS3.AI, DefiLlama tracking shows the recovery took roughly 95 days from the shock to July 22. The exploit released about $292 million in unbacked rsETH and triggered a $13 billion decline across DeFi in 48 hours.

data.title: Lombard Finance Launches Bitcoin Onchain Credit Strategy With Flow Traders as Pilot Partner
data.description: Lombard Finance has launched the Bitcoin Onchain Credit Strategy with Flow Traders as its first pilot partner. According to NS3.AI, the solution allows Flow Traders to borrow stablecoins for market making without posting on-chain collateral.

data.title: Jiang Zhuoer Says Rising Oil Prices Pose Major Risk
data.description:
Jiang Zhuoer said on X that the market is ignoring a major "gray rhino" risk: continued increases in international oil prices. According to Odaily, he said Brent crude has rebounded to around $95 and that oil prices are likely to rise further amid the current standoff between the United States and Iran over the Strait of Hormuz.

He said that if transportation through the Strait of Hormuz remains disrupted, it would have a catastrophic impact on the global economy. He also said global commercial and government crude reserves have been heavily drawn down, and that total U.S. crude inventories, including commercial stockpiles and the Strategic Petroleum Reserve, have fallen to their lowest level since 1984. He added that if international oil prices rise back above $100, market sentiment may deteriorate quickly.

data.title: Federal Reserve's July Meeting Faces Unusual Uncertainty, Nick Timiraos Says
data.description:
Nick Timiraos said on July 23 local time that the Federal Reserve's July policy meeting is set to be one of the hardest to predict in recent years. According to Odaily, rising oil prices, higher U.S. tariff policy risks, and support from some officials for a rate hike are challenging the consensus to keep rates unchanged.

The market widely expects the Federal Open Market Committee meeting on July 28 and July 29 to keep the policy rate unchanged at 3.50% to 3.75%. However, the outcome would not end the internal debate, and some officials have already begun laying the groundwork for another rate hike later this year.

At a previous meeting, the Fed's 18 officials were divided on whether another rate hike is needed this year, with half expecting a hike and half seeing no need for changes. UBS chief U.S. economist Jonathan Pingle said Fed Chair Kevin Warsh could become a key figure in determining the policy direction.

data.title: South African Reserve Bank: Recent Oil Price Volatility Shows Upside and Downside Risks
data.description: According to Jin10, the South African Reserve Bank said recent oil price volatility shows upside and downside risks, depending on how the Middle East conflict develops.

data.title: South African Reserve Bank: 2026 Oil Price at $100 a Barrel in Updated Negative Scenario
data.description: South African Reserve Bank said that in its updated negative scenario, oil prices in 2026 are $100 a barrel, inflation remains above target, and an additional rate hike is needed this year. According to Jin10,

data.title: AI TRENDS | ByteDance Launches Seed STEM Scientist Program for 100 Scholars
data.description: ByteDance has formally launched its Seed STEM scientist program, inviting 100 leading scholars in frontier science fields to work on industry-academia-research projects. According to Odaily, the program is led by the ByteDance Seed Edge research team and provides computing resources and competitive pay. It offers two participation roles: scientific adviser and doctoral intern. The first project cycle is expected to last about six months, with work based in Haidian District, Beijing. Applications are open until September 30, 2026.

data.title: BitMEX to Shut Down Exchange on September 23 After Strategic Review
data.description:
BitMEX's official announcement stated that the exchange will close on September 23, 2026 at 04:00 UTC, with new account registrations halted immediately. The board of HDR Global Trading Limited decided to close the exchange following a strategic review of the business and the broader crypto industry.
From August 26, risk limits will stop users from placing new positions, allowing only position reduction. BitMEX will force close remaining open positions at the Closure Time. All staked BMEX tokens have been unstaked and are available in holder accounts.
KYC'd users who fail to withdraw by the Closure Time will be charged a monthly fee of $50 equivalent or 1% per annum, whichever is greater. The exchange warned users to be vigilant against phishing attempts and confirmed all assets exceed liabilities per its Proof of Reserves and Liabilities page.

data.title: Capitole Macro: ECB Gives Few Hints on Its Next Move
data.description: Jack Allen-Reynolds, an economist at Capitole Macro, said the European Central Bank's decision to keep rates unchanged was not surprising and that the accompanying press release gave no clear signal on its next move. According to Jin10, he said the comment on policy prospects remained neutral and noted that energy prices were close to the ECB's baseline assumption. Allen-Reynolds said the ECB may keep rates unchanged this year if energy prices fall in the coming months, but that it is very likely to raise rates again if energy prices keep rising. He also said ECB President Christine Lagarde may give a hint at a news conference if policymakers expect a rate hike in September.

data.title: Kennedy faces test as cyclospora outbreak tops 7,000 cases in Michigan
data.description: According to CNBC, Health and Human Services Secretary Robert F. Kennedy Jr. is under pressure as U.S. health agencies try to contain a cyclospora outbreak that has sickened at least 7,000 people in Michigan alone, while the Centers for Disease Control and Prevention’s official count remains near 4,200 cases. The Food and Drug Administration and the CDC have narrowed their focus to shredded iceberg lettuce from Taylor Farms, after the FDA briefly said the product tested positive for cyclospora before later calling that result a false positive. The outbreak response has drawn criticism from former officials and food safety experts, who say federal funding cuts, staff losses and delayed communications have left the system less able to investigate and contain foodborne illness. HHS said the FDA has been transparent with Taylor Farms and that the corrected lab result does not change its findings. The CDC has received reports of more than 4,000 laboratory-confirmed cases and is aware of more than 7,400 additional unconfirmed cases, many in Michigan and Ohio.

data.title: Fidelity International: ECB Pause Is Temporary, 25-Basis-Point Hike Seen in September
data.description: Fidelity International London eurozone economist Conor Parle said the European Central Bank kept rates unchanged at today's meeting, but the pause is likely temporary. According to Jin10, Parle said that, with rising commodity prices and the latest escalation in tensions in the Middle East, the ECB may be in a better position after updating its economic forecasts in September to raise rates by another 25 basis points to the upper end of the neutral range and send a clear signal that it remains committed to price stability.

data.title: Empery Digital Completes $20 Million Preferred Stock Investment in Cardinal Data Power
data.description: Empery Digital said it completed a $20 million strategic preferred stock investment in AI data center developer Cardinal Data Power and now holds about 8% of the company. According to Odaily, the investment is part of CDP's $70 million Series A financing, and the funds will be used for construction of its first AI data center campus in West Texas.

data.title: Strive Vice President Joe Burnett Says Self-Custodied Bitcoin Is Irreplaceable
data.description: Strive Vice President Joe Burnett said on X that self-custodied Bitcoin is irreplaceable. According to Odaily, he added that a less recognized benefit of public companies and institutions adopting Bitcoin is that they can devote significant resources to maintaining, testing, and strengthening the protocol for the next several hundred years.

data.title: Ethereum(ETH) Drops Below 1,900 USDT with a 1.19% Decrease in 24 Hours
data.description: On Jul 23, 2026, 12:46 PM(UTC). According to Binance Market Data, Ethereum has dropped below 1,900 USDT and is now trading at 1,898.51001 USDT, with a narrowed 1.19% decrease in 24 hours.

data.title: PRECIOUS METALS | Spot Gold Slips Below $4,070 an Ounce, Down 1.45%
data.description: Spot gold edged lower after the data release, falling below $4,070 an ounce and down 1.45% on the day. According to Jin10, the move came after the data was published.

data.title: Bitcoin Trader Closes 300 BTC Short Positions and Books $157,000 Profit
data.description:
A trader known as "Set 10 Big Goals First" has begun closing short positions and taking profits. According to Foresight News, the trader closed 300 BTC over the past 12 minutes and realized a profit of $157,000.

The trader still holds 2,379.23 BTC in positions, with unrealized gains of $1.34 million.
